Rabbi Azarya began: “We sought to heal Babylon, but it was not healed; forsake it, and let us go, each to his land, as its judgment reaches the heavens and rises to the sky” (Jeremiah 51:9). “We sought to heal Babylon” – in the generation of Enosh. “But it was not healed – in the generation of the Flood. Forsake it, and let us go, each to his land – “the entire earth was of one language.”
will raise up against. Babylon, and against them that. dwell in the midst of them that rise up against me, a destroying wind ;

The destruction of Babylon was foreseen by Jeremiah (Jer. 51:37), Nephi (1 1 Ne. 14:15-16; 22:14), and Joseph Smith (D&C 1:16), among others. We read in Jeremiah of this impressive prophecy: "So Jeremiah wrote in a book all the evil that should come upon Babylon. . . . And Jeremiah said to Seraiah, When . . . thou hast made an end of reading this book, . . . thou shalt bind a stone to it, and…
